title: Human Brain Vascular Pericytes (HBVP) Culture and Plating
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bjk18","text":"This protocol implements the culture of Human Brain Vascular Pericytes (HBVP) and is suitable for several research techniques, including immunohistochemistry, flow cytometry, and Protein or RNA analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"cejan","text":"Please read the whole protocol before starting the procedure.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"35a24","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"hjok","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"f5qap","text":"It is pivotal that all the reagents used in this protocol are warmed at   prior contact with cells. steps: Prepare and warm 50 mL of Pericyte Complete Medium (PCM, see materials section) at 37 °C for 00:30:00 .

Retrieve form -130 °C the Human Brain Vascular Pericytes (HBVP) and place them at 37 °C for 00:02:00 . As frozen HBVP contain DMSO, cell exposure to this reagent at 37 °C should be minimal. Therefore, incubate only as long as necessary until the aliquot is in liquid form.

Place 1 mL (~500.000 cells) of HBVP in a 75cm² Cell Culture Flask filled with 9 mL of PCM. Cell culture can also be performed in smaller Cell Culture Flasks if fast confluence or reduced cell number is required. In those cases, adjust the PCM volume (i.e 4 mL for 25cm² Cell Culture Flask).
Perform gently crosswise movements to spread the cells over the container's surface, and visually check cell dispersion under a microscope.

Incubate the cells at 37 °C until reaching a confluence of 70-80% before proceeding with cell plating. In a 75cm² Cell Culture Flask it is expected that 70-80% confluence results in 6-7 million cells . When HBVP are taken from -130 frozen aliquots, the PCM must be replaced daily during the first two days to eliminate any traces of DMSO (see step 20). After, replace the medium every two days until the desired confluence is reached.

The steps contained in this section (Glass Coverslip Preparation) are required if the experimental approach entails immunohistochemistry . Otherwise, If mounting in coverslips is not necessary (i.e. protein extraction), the researcher can proceed to cell detachment.
Slowly thaw aliquoted Matrigel by transferring it from -20 °C to a recipient with minced ice.

Dilute 1:30 the Matrigel in On ice cooled DPBS according to the required volume per wheel ( 200 µL for 12 wheels plate, 400 mL for 6 wheels plate ) and filter the solution using a sterile Syringe and a Syringe filter.
Place cold (stored at -20 °C ) Glass Coverslips in each wheel and add 1 mL 70% ethanol for 00:05:00 with subsequent suction.
On ice , add 200 µL for 12 wheels plate or 400 mL for 6 wheels plate of filtered matrigel and incubate the plate at 37 °C for 00:30:00 .
When matrigel incubation is finished, wash the wheels three times with PCM ( 200 µL for 12 wheels plate, 500 mL for 6 wheels plate) and subsequently, aspirate the media.
Before starting the procedure, make sure that PCM , DPBS , and Trypsin-EDTA solution are warmed at 37 °C for 00:30:00
When cells have reached 70-80% confluence (6-7 million cells), take them from the incubator, carefully suction the PCM , and wash 1 time with DPBS.

After suctioning DPBS, add 3 mL of trypsin-EDTA solution and incubate at 37 °C for 00:05:00 to achieve cell detachment from the flask. If culture is performed in a smaller Cell Culture Flask, adjust the trypsin-EDTA volume accordingly. For 25cm² Cell Culture Flask, 1.5 mL of the solution is enough.

Check under the microscope that cell detachment was achieved, and add 9 mL of PCM (three times the trypsin-EDTA volume) to stop the chemical reaction. If culture is performed in a smaller Cell Culture Flask, adjust the PCM volume to be three times the trypsin-EDTA volume.
Repeatedly pipette the medium contained in the Cell Culture Flask to ensure complete detachment of cells that may still be adhered to the surface.
Suction the whole culture media containing the detached cells, and place it in a 50 mL sterile Falcon tube .
To separate the cells from the PCM and remaining trypsin-EDTA solution, centrifugate at 1000 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes, Acceleration/deceleration speed= 6

When centrifugation is finished and cells are attached to the bottom of the Falcon tube, carefully remove the supernatant and add PCM with gently pipetting to resuspend the cells (check cases) . Known cell number This protocol estimates that under a confluence of 70-80% in a 75cm2 Cell Culture Flask, the researcher can retrieve a total of 7 million cells. In this scenario, cells are resuspended in 7 ml PCM to obtain 1 million cells/ml. Cell counting If required or desired, the researcher can conduct at this point manual or automatic cell counting according to equipment availability. This is usually done by 1:1 incubation of 10 ul of Trypan Blue and a 1/10 diluted cell sample. Then, resuspension in PCM is performed according to the desired cell concentration per milliliter.

At this point, cells are ready to be plated (See next section) or frozen. If the researcher wishes to freeze the cells, they must be resuspended in PCM without FBS and add 20% v/v Dimethyl Sulfoxide (DMSO) .

To freeze the cells, add 1 mL (1 million cells) of cell suspension in 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes, and place them in a recipient filled with isopentane. Transfer the recipient to a -80 °C refrigerator for 24:00:00 , and finally place them at -130 °C for further use. During the freezing and thaw cycles, a loss of ~50% of cells is expected. Therefore, it is recommended to freeze 1 million cells, in order to seed at least 500.000 cells in the next passage. (see steps 2-3).

Add 1 mL (for 6 wheels plate) or 750 mL (for 12 wheel plates) of PCM to each wheel containing the Matrigel Coated Glass Coverslips (see Glass Coverslip Preparation section), followed by 0.5 mL (for 6 wheels plate) or 250 mL (for 12 whell plate) of PCM-suspended pericytes .
Incubate the wheel plate at 37 °C until the desired confluence is achieved. 70-80% Confluency in 6 or 12 wheels plates is achieved in 1-3 days depending on the plated cell density.
creator: Daniel Manrique-Castano
